reasoning: The informal statement claims Aut(ℚ) has exactly one element. In Mathlib, `RingAut ℚ` is defined as `RingEquiv ℚ ℚ` (the group of ring automorphisms), and since ℚ is a field, ring automorphisms coincide with field automorphisms — which is the intended meaning of "Aut(ℚ)" (the only interpretation that makes the claim true, since additive automorphisms of ℚ form ℚ*). The statement `Nat.card (RingAut ℚ) = 1` asserts exactly that this group has cardinality 1, i.e., a single element. The group structure is inherent in `RingAut` (Mathlib provides a `Group` instance), so "is a group with a single element" is faithfully captured. This is a logically equivalent formalization.
